analysis_summary: The proposed method produced 8 finding(s) from 8 analysis obligation(s).

findings.summary: DMI/JTAG and simulation debug request paths reach debug module controls without a visible top-level jtag_unlock/authentication guard; debug NOC slave access to the debug module is also reachable, although dm_master system-bus responses are tied off.
findings.vulnerability_category: Issue DMI debug requests/responses, trigger hart debug_req_o, and access debug module slave bus transactions / debug_req_o, debug_req/debug_req_valid, dm_slave_req/we/addr/wdata/rdata, dm_master AXI/NOC bridge / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: External JTAG/DTM inputs tck_i/tms_i/trst_ni/td_i, SimDTM/SimJTAG requester, and debug NOC requester via buf_ariane_debug_noc2_* Operation: Issue DMI debug requests/responses, trigger hart debug_req_o, and access debug module slave bus transactions Object: debug_req_o, debug_req/debug_req_valid, dm_slave_req/we/addr/wdata/rdata, dm_master AXI/NOC bridge Expected guard: debug authorization or jtag_unlock/authenticated state before debug request controls hart debug entry or debug-module/memory-facing transactions Observed behavior: dmi_jtag receives jtag_hash/okey_hash/ikey_hash and outputs jtag_unlock, but debug_req/debug_req_valid are wired toward dm_top without a visible jtag_unlock guard. Under RISCV_FESVR_SIM, SimDTM directly drives debug_req_valid and debug_req fields. The debug NOC AXI-lite bridge drives dm_master, converted by axi2mem to dm_slave_* into dm_top. dm_master_gnt/r_valid/r_rdata are tied off, limiting unsupported system bus access, but not the DMI/debug-module slave path. Path: JTAG/SimDTM/SimJTAG or debug NOC -> dmi_jtag/SimDTM or noc_axilite_bridge -> debug_req/debug_req_valid or dm_master -> axi2mem -> dm_slave_* -> dm_top -> debug_req_o Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: External or simulation debug requester may control hart debug entry or debug module state without visible authentication mediation.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: Full dm_top instance lines were not fully present in observations, but tool/model observations identify dm_top receiving debug_req_valid/debug_req and outputting debug_req_o around lines 353-388.

findings.summary: reglk_wrapper lock state is overwriteable with raw wdata when selected lock bits are not asserted; no set-only sticky behavior is visible, and jtag_unlock/rst_9 clear all lock memory.
findings.vulnerability_category: Write, clear, or update lock bits / reglk_mem and exported reglk_ctrl_o/reglk_ctrl vector controlling wrapper register writes / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: Software-visible AXI requester through reglk NOC AXI-lite bridge, with JTAG unlock override Operation: Write, clear, or update lock bits Object: reglk_mem and exported reglk_ctrl_o/reglk_ctrl vector controlling wrapper register writes Expected guard: Lock state should be sticky or clears/overwrites should require authorized jtag_unlock/equivalent privileged condition Observed behavior: reglk_ctrl_o is concatenated from reglk_mem. en is only en_acct && acct_ctrl_i. Reset condition ~(rst_ni && ~jtag_unlock && ~rst_9) clears all reglk_mem entries. During en && we, address cases assign reglk_mem[n] <= wdata when selected reglk_ctrl_i bit is clear; writing 0 can clear previously set bits in that situation. No OR-with-prior-value/set-only update is visible. Path: reglk NOC -> noc_axilite_bridge -> reglk_axi_req -> reglk_wrapper AXI-lite interface -> reglk_mem -> reglk_ctrl_o -> peripheral wrapper reglk_ctrl_i consumers Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: A requester with reglk access can clear or weaken register locks before corresponding lock controls are asserted; unlock/reset paths clear lock vector and may defeat lifecycle stickiness.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: Complete reglk_wrapper read-side was truncated, but write/reset behavior is sufficient for sticky-lock candidate.

findings.summary: Access-control policy defaults open, can be forced by we_flag_0, and is self-gated by the same acc_ctrl matrix it configures; at least one observed peripheral path (PLIC state machine) lacks a visible acc_ctrl_c guard.
findings.vulnerability_category: Configure access policy and gate peripheral requests / acct_mem/acc_ctrl_o, acc_ctrl_c[priv_lvl_i][peripheral], rom_req, wrapper acct_ctrl_i, PLIC request path / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: Software-visible AXI/NOC requester writing acct_wrapper and current requester selected by priv_lvl_i Operation: Configure access policy and gate peripheral requests Object: acct_mem/acc_ctrl_o, acc_ctrl_c[priv_lvl_i][peripheral], rom_req, wrapper acct_ctrl_i, PLIC request path Expected guard: Policy updates only by authorized privilege/locked configuration; peripheral access consistently gated by acc_ctrl_c indexed by requester privilege Observed behavior: acct_wrapper reset sets acct_mem entries to 32'hffffffff, yielding default-open policy. acc_ctrl_o ORs acct_mem[0] with {8{we_flag}}, allowing we_flag_0 to force access bits. Writes are allowed when en_acct && acct_ctrl_i && we and selected reglk_ctrl bits permit, with acct_ctrl_i sourced from acc_ctrl_c[priv_lvl_i][6], making policy update self-referential. Top-level gates ROM and many wrappers with acc_ctrl_c, but observed PLIC request state machine asserts plic_req.valid from AXI valid/ready without visible acc_ctrl_c term. Path: acct NOC -> acct_axilite_bridge -> acct_wrapper -> acc_ctrl -> acc_ctrl_c[priv_lvl_i][j] -> ROM/wrapper gates; PLIC AXI-lite path lacks visible acc_ctrl_c gate Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: Lower-privilege or unauthorized software may inherit broad default access, force policy bits, or encounter inconsistent peripheral mediation.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: Full acct_wrapper write cases and all peripheral wrappers were truncated; however search observations list acc_ctrl_c consumers and PLIC path excerpt lacks guard.

findings.summary: pkt_wrapper exposes fuse_rdata_i and pkey_loc through AXI read mux under only acct_ctrl_i/reglk read-mask bits; fuse_req_o/fuse_addr_o are writable when acct_ctrl_i permits and no separate privilege/lock authorization is visible.
findings.vulnerability_category: Request/read fuse or key-location data / fuse_req_o, fuse_addr_o, fuse_rdata_i/fuse_rdata, pkey_loc, AXI rdata / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: Software-visible AXI/NOC requester to pkt_wrapper Operation: Request/read fuse or key-location data Object: fuse_req_o, fuse_addr_o, fuse_rdata_i/fuse_rdata, pkey_loc, AXI rdata Expected guard: Fuse reads and address selection require authorized access policy/lock/privilege and should not expose fuse contents to unauthorized AXI/NOC requesters Observed behavior: pkt_wrapper uses en = en_acct && acct_ctrl_i. On en && we, address 0 writes fuse_req_o from wdata[0], address 1 writes fuse_addr_o from wdata. Read mux initializes rdata to fuse_rdata_i when en, returns pkey_loc halves when reglk_ctrl_i[4]/[5] are not set, returns fuse_rdata_i at address 4 when reglk_ctrl_i[6] is not set, and default path can also leave rdata as fuse_rdata_i unless fuse_addr_o <= 110 forces zero. Top-level connects pkt acct_ctrl_i to acc_ctrl_c[priv_lvl_i][5] and routes responses over NOC. Path: pkt NOC -> noc_axilite_bridge -> pkt_axi_req -> pkt_wrapper -> fuse_req_o/fuse_addr_o -> fuse_rdata_i/pkey_loc -> rdata -> pkt AXI/NOC response Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: Authorized-by-default or misconfigured acc_ctrl/reglk state can directly expose fuse data/key-location values to software-visible AXI/NOC reads.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: Top-level source of fuse_rdata was not included; authorization of external fuse memory is unknown.

findings.summary: dma_wrapper configuration registers are software-visible and gated by acct_ctrl_i/reglk_ctrl_i; PMP inputs exist, but provided observations do not show actual address-range enforcement or downstream transfer outputs, so range permission cannot be confirmed.
findings.vulnerability_category: Configure and start DMA transfer / core_lock_reg, start_reg, length_reg, source_addr_lsb/msb_reg, dest_addr_lsb/msb_reg, done/end/valid and downstream transfer / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: Software-visible AXI requester configuring DMA wrapper; DMA engine as possible bus master Operation: Configure and start DMA transfer Object: core_lock_reg, start_reg, length_reg, source_addr_lsb/msb_reg, dest_addr_lsb/msb_reg, done/end/valid and downstream transfer Expected guard: DMA config/start/address/length writes require acct_ctrl_i/reglk_ctrl_i and address-range/PMP-like checks; config should be blocked during active/locked transfer Observed behavior: dma_wrapper declares PMP inputs pmpcfg_i/pmpaddr_i and config registers. AXI-lite en is en_acct && acct_ctrl_i. Reset/rst_8 clears configuration registers. Observations are truncated before the write-case and any PMP/range/active-transfer logic. Top-level connects reglk_ctrl[8], acct_ctrl_i(acc_ctrl_c[priv_lvl_i][8]), pmpcfg_i/pmpaddr_i, we_flag_3, and NOC AXI-lite bridge. Path: dma NOC -> noc_axilite_bridge -> dma_axi_req -> dma_wrapper config registers -> potential DMA transfer path Closure verdict: Related RTL evidence exists, but some core evidence slots remain incomplete.
findings.security_impact: If no hidden range/active checks exist, software could program arbitrary DMA transfers; current evidence is insufficient to classify as violation.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: Write-case details after dma_wrapper.sv:124, PMP/range checking, active transfer state, and downstream bus master wiring are not provided.

findings.summary: Most wrapper instances receive acc_ctrl_c[priv_lvl_i][peripheral], but observed PLIC request generation lacks an acc_ctrl_c guard, and NOC bridges feed AXI request signals directly before wrapper-level gating; response behavior on denied requests is not fully established.
findings.vulnerability_category: Route reads/writes to peripherals over NOC/AXI / Peripheral request/response paths for BOOTROM, RST, RNG, AES0/1/2, RSA, SHA256, HMAC, PKT, ACCT, REGLK, DMA, PLIC and other protected registers / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: CPU/NOC/AXI requester with priv_lvl_i Operation: Route reads/writes to peripherals over NOC/AXI Object: Peripheral request/response paths for BOOTROM, RST, RNG, AES0/1/2, RSA, SHA256, HMAC, PKT, ACCT, REGLK, DMA, PLIC and other protected registers Expected guard: Each peripheral request should be gated by acc_ctrl_c[priv_lvl_i][peripheral] or equivalent address/privilege guard; denied requests should not leak response data Observed behavior: Search observations show acc_ctrl_c used for ROM and many wrappers, including rst/rng/aes/rsa/sha/hmac/pkt/acct/reglk/dma. However the PLIC state machine excerpt asserts plic_req.valid based on plic_master valid/ready with no visible acc_ctrl_c term. NOC bridges for wrappers drive AXI request channels directly; wrapper internals often gate en = en_acct && acct_ctrl_i, but full response behavior for denied access is not shown. Path: NOC/AXI peripheral request routing -> noc_axilite_bridge -> peripheral AXI req/resp and wrapper acct_ctrl_i gates Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: At least PLIC appears ungated by central access matrix, and default-open policy weakens protection across gated paths; denied-response leakage cannot be ruled out.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: Full request generation/response mux for every peripheral was not provided; PLIC wrapper/top-level gating outside excerpt could not be excluded, but no evidence was observed.

findings.summary: External we_flag inputs can force or alter protected control behavior without visible testmode/authorization qualification: we_flag_0 forces acc_ctrl outputs, we_flag_1 ORs AES0 reglk_ctrl_i, we_flag_3 enters DMA wrapper, and we_flag_4 enters dmi_jtag.
findings.vulnerability_category: Override or enable protected register/control behavior / acc_ctrl policy outputs, DMA protected registers, AES0 lock controls, JTAG/debug authentication path / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: External/top-level write-enable flag source we_flag_0..we_flag_4 Operation: Override or enable protected register/control behavior Object: acc_ctrl policy outputs, DMA protected registers, AES0 lock controls, JTAG/debug authentication path Expected guard: we_flag overrides should not bypass reglk_ctrl/acct_ctrl/privilege/test authorization and should be qualified by testmode or secure lifecycle state Observed behavior: Top-level exposes we_flag_0..we_flag_4 as inputs. acct_wrapper ORs acct_mem[0] with {8{we_flag}}, forcing access-control output bits. Top-level ORs we_flag_1 into AES0 reglk_ctrl_i. DMA wrapper receives we_flag_3, though truncated evidence does not show exact use. dmi_jtag receives we_flag_4. No observed connection qualifies these flags with testmode_i at top-level. Path: we_flag_0..4 top-level inputs -> dmi_jtag/acct_wrapper/aes0_wrapper/dma_wrapper -> protected control/policy/register paths Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: External flag source may bypass or modify normal permission/lock checks, granting access or altering protected debug/DMA/AES behavior.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: Exact uses of we_flag_3 inside dma_wrapper and we_flag_4 inside dmi_jtag are not visible; no evidence was provided for we_flag_2 use beyond top-level input.

findings.summary: Software/NOC-accessible reset wrapper can assert per-peripheral resets that clear security-relevant state; reset control is only gated by acc_ctrl_c[priv_lvl_i][12]/reglk_ctrl and debug_mode_i, while downstream resets clear locks, access policy, DMA config, and fuse controls.
findings.vulnerability_category: Assert reset or clear protected state / reglk_ctrl/reglk_mem, acc_ctrl/acct_mem, DMA config/status, pkt fuse_req/fuse_addr and peripheral state affected by rst_1..rst_13 / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: Software/NOC requester reaching rst_wrapper and external/global reset sources Operation: Assert reset or clear protected state Object: reglk_ctrl/reglk_mem, acc_ctrl/acct_mem, DMA config/status, pkt fuse_req/fuse_addr and peripheral state affected by rst_1..rst_13 Expected guard: Software/external resets of security wrappers should require privileged lifecycle authorization and should not clear locks/policy or leave unsafe stale/observable state Observed behavior: Top-level rst_wrapper is NOC-accessible through rst_axilite_bridge and receives acct_ctrl_i(acc_ctrl_c[priv_lvl_i][12]), reglk_ctrl_i(reglk_ctrl[12]), and debug_mode_i. It outputs rst_1..rst_13 to wrappers. reglk_wrapper clears all reglk_mem on rst_9; acct_wrapper resets acct_mem to 32'hffffffff on rst_6; dma_wrapper clears config registers on rst_8; pkt_wrapper clears fuse_req_o/fuse_addr_o on rst_5. Because acct reset default is all ones and reglk reset default is zero, reset can re-open policy/clear locks if rst_wrapper access is not strictly authorized. Path: rst NOC -> rst_axilite_bridge -> rst_wrapper -> rst_5/rst_6/rst_8/rst_9 -> pkt/acct/dma/reglk sequential reset branches Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: Authorized-by-default or lower-privileged reset access can clear locks/policy and return system to permissive state, undermining lifecycle protection and residue guarantees.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: rst_wrapper internal write authorization and exact reset generation logic were not provided; classification is based on its visible NOC accessibility, gate inputs, and downstream reset effects.

global_uncertainty: No closure-level missing evidence was recorded beyond the visible source scope limitations.
